title: convolvulus pes-tigridis
intro: Convolvulus pes-tigridis, also known as catgut vine or wild sweetpea, is a perennial climber native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its delicate pink to purple flowers and twining stems. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese and Ayurvedic medicine, where it is used to treat digestive issues, fever, and inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Convolvulus pes-tigridis include its potential to alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders, reduce fever, and support wound healing, making it a popular herb in natural remedies. Herbal preparations of Convolvulus pes-tigridis typically involve drying the leaves and flowers, which can be made into teas, infusions, or added to tinctures and ointments for various medicinal applications.
